name: rizin
title: rizin
summary: reverse engineering framework and command-line toolset
description:
reverse engineering framework and command-line toolset
Rizin is a fork of the radare2 reverse engineering framework with a
focus on usability, working features and code cleanliness.
.
Rizin is portable and it can be used to analyze binaries, disassemble
code, debug programs, as a forensics tool, as a scriptable command-line
hexadecimal editor able to open disk files, and much more!

commands.name: rizin
commands.description: Advanced commandline hexadecimal editor, disassembler and debugger

arguments.description: Run 'aaa' command before prompt or patch to analyze all referenced code
arguments.raw: -A  Run 'aaa' command before prompt or patch to analyze all referenced code

arguments.description: Set the assembly architecture.(x86, ppc, arm, mips, bf, java, ...)
arguments.raw: -a  Set the assembly architecture.(x86, ppc, arm, mips, bf, java, ...)

arguments.description: Set the base address for Position-Independent Executables (PIE)
arguments.raw: -B  Set the base address for Position-Independent Executables (PIE)

arguments.description: Set the assembly bitness. (16, 32, 64)
arguments.raw: -b  Set the assembly bitness. (16, 32, 64)

arguments.description: Specify a file in the format "host:port" to run Rizin remotely through HTTP
arguments.raw: -C  Specify a file in the format "host:port" to run Rizin remotely through HTTP

arguments.description: Debug the executable 'file' or running process 'pid'
arguments.raw: -d  Debug the executable 'file' or running process 'pid'

arguments.description: Set block size to be equal to file size
arguments.raw: -f  Set block size to be equal to file size

arguments.description: Show help messages, with -hh showing detailed help
arguments.raw: -h  Show help messages, with -hh showing detailed help

arguments.description: Show help messages, with -hh showing detailed help
arguments.raw: -hh  Show help messages, with -hh showing detailed help

arguments.description: Run script file. Before the file is loaded
arguments.raw: -I  Run script file. Before the file is loaded

arguments.description: Run the commands from the script file
arguments.raw: -i  Run the commands from the script file

arguments.description: Set the assembly operating system
arguments.raw: -k  Set the assembly operating system

arguments.description: List supported IO plugins
arguments.raw: -L  List supported IO plugins

arguments.description: Load the specified plugin file
arguments.raw: -l  Load the specified plugin file

arguments.description: Disable demangling
arguments.raw: -M  Disable demangling

arguments.description: Map file at given address
arguments.raw: -m  Map file at given address

arguments.description: Control loading of RzBin info, with -nn only loading binary structures
arguments.raw: -n  Control loading of RzBin info, with -nn only loading binary structures

arguments.description: Disable loading any scripts or plugins
arguments.raw: -NN  Disable loading any scripts or plugins

arguments.description: Control loading of RzBin info, with -nn only loading binary structures
arguments.raw: -nn  Control loading of RzBin info, with -nn only loading binary structures

arguments.description: Set project file
arguments.raw: -p  Set project file

arguments.description: Quiet mode, suppresses prompt and quits after script execution
arguments.raw: -q  Quiet mode, suppresses prompt and quits after script execution

arguments.description: Quit before showing the prompt. Right after all the -e -c and -i arguments are
arguments.raw: -qq  Quit before showing the prompt. Right after all the -e -c and -i arguments are

arguments.description: Specify custom rz-run directives without having to create a rz-run profile
arguments.raw: -R  Specify custom rz-run directives without having to create a rz-run profile

arguments.description: Specify dbg.profile rz-run profile to use when spawning a program for debugging
arguments.raw: -r  Specify dbg.profile rz-run profile to use when spawning a program for debugging

arguments.description: Start seeking at this address
arguments.raw: -s  Start seeking at this address

arguments.description: Avoid computing the file hashes
arguments.raw: -T  Avoid computing the file hashes

arguments.description: Set bin.filter=false to load rbin info without filtering names
arguments.raw: -u  Set bin.filter=false to load rbin info without filtering names

arguments.description: Show rizin library versions
arguments.raw: -V  Show rizin library versions

arguments.description: Show version information and exit (Use -qv to get just the version number)
arguments.raw: -v  Show version information and exit (Use -qv to get just the version number)

arguments.description: Open in write mode
arguments.raw: -w  Open in write mode

arguments.description: Same as -e bin.usextr=false, do not use extract plugins, useful for dyldcache
arguments.raw: -X  Same as -e bin.usextr=false, do not use extract plugins, useful for dyldcache

arguments.description: Open the file without the execute flag
arguments.raw: -x  Open the file without the execute flag

arguments.description: do not load strings or load them even in raw
arguments.raw: -zz  do not load strings or load them even in raw

commands.name: rz-diff
commands.description: Compare files and find binary differences.

commands.name: rz-gg
commands.description: rizin frontend for RzEgg, compile programs into tiny binaries for different architectures.

commands.name: rz-run
commands.description: rizin utility to run programs in custom environments

command_examples: rizin [-ACdfLMnNqStuvwzX] [-P patch] [-p prj] [-a arch] [-b bits] [-i file] [-s addr ]
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use rizin because reverse engineering framework and command-line toolset.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use rizin as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `rizin [-ACdfLMnNqStuvwzX] [-P patch] [-p prj] [-a arch] [-b bits] [-i file] [-s addr ]` as documented upstream for rizin.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `rizin -A /bin/ls` as documented upstream for rizin.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `rizin -w /bin/ls` as documented upstream for rizin.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `rizin -d /bin/ls -l /tmp` as documented upstream for rizin.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair rizin with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `rizin --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: rizin -- Advanced commandline hexadecimal editor, disassembler and debugger
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install rizin. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `rizin -A /bin/ls`. Further invocations from upstream: `rizin -w /bin/ls`; `rizin -d /bin/ls -l /tmp`. reverse engineering framework and command-line toolset.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: reverse engineering framework and command-line toolset.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: rizin -- Advanced commandline hexadecimal editor, disassembler and debugger.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install rizin
Install with: sudo apt install rizin
